title: Audit apt periodic cache cleanup policy
summary: Review apt periodic cleanup settings before adding any automated package-cache retention remedy.

evidence.relevance: The apt cache is larger than the package-cache review threshold.
Documents the periodic apt cleanup knobs reviewers should verify.

compatibility: Debian Bookworm hosts using apt package management.
Raspberry Pi write-sensitive USB or SSD profiles where cache churn should be explicit.
Not applicable to hosts that disable apt periodic services or use another package manager.

notes: Automated cache deletion may increase future network use and should remain advisory until reviewed.

rollback: Restore the previous apt periodic configuration files from backup if reviewers later promote a config change.
Downloaded package files can be reacquired through apt when needed.

proposedChecks.reason: Check whether apt periodic timers are active.
Measure current package cache size before any cleanup.

proposedApplyCommands.reason: Potential manual cleanup command for obsolete package files after review.

tests: Unit test parser coverage for apt periodic config snippets.
Fixture-backed suggestion test for large apt cache with periodic cleanup disabled.

promptChanges: Add an apt periodic cleanup example to the research prompt if this candidate is promoted.
